title: A Fault-Tolerance Threshold for Gated Agentic Computation: Reliable Long-Horizon Work from Unreliable Executors
description: A theoretical model and self-contained simulation showing that, above a threshold, the reliable horizon of an agentic system is governed not by the agent's raw error rate but by the shared blind-spot ('stealth') mass of its verification stack. The paper proves: a fault-tolerance threshold giving logarithmic verification overhead below the threshold; a horizon ceiling of 1/stealth-mass imposed by correlated gate blind spots; a decomposition of the stealth mass into a diversity-reducible part and a specification-bound irreducible floor; and a Young-Daly-style cost-optimal checkpoint interval. Includes a validated, seed-independent Python script (simulate.py) reproducing every numerical result and both figures.
